channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania Embassy staff in France farewell Ambassador Ali Mwadini after his new appointment
item.description: Staff at the Embassy of Tanzania in France have held a farewell ceremony for former Ambassador Ali J. Mwadini after his appointment as Secretary to the President of the United Republic of Tanzania, Dr...

item.title: Army seizes Dawlah Islamiyah weapons facility in Lanao del Sur
item.description: Army troops from the 64th Infantry Battalion raided a suspected firearms manufacturing site of the Dawlah Islamiyah-Maute Group in Barangay Pabrica, Marogong, Lanao del Sur over the weekend. Acting on...

item.title: Comelec clarifies that BSKE preparations and budget can still be used if polls are postponed
item.description: Kasama ng Commission on Elections (Comelec), sinabi ni Chairman George Erwin Garcia na ang mga paghahanda nito para sa 2026 Barangay at Sangguniang Kabataan Elections (BSKE) ay hindi mawawala kahit ma...

item.title: U.S. Producer Price Growth Slows in March Despite Rising Energy Costs
item.description: U.S. producer prices rose by 0.5 per cent in March, below economists' expectations of a 1.1 per cent increase, according to the Bureau of Labor Statistics. The rise was mainly driven by higher energy ...

item.title: PM’s cautious stance on Trump’s rhetoric amid US-Israel-Iran conflict
item.description: In recent weeks, as conflict between the United States and Israel escalates against Iran, Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has faced criticism for his careful and measured statements. Despite Donald Tr...

item.title: Marcos Receives Ambassadors from Oman and Six Other Nations, Highlights Cooperation and OFW Support
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. on Tuesday welcomed Oman’s Ambassador Nasser bin Said bin Abdullah Al Manwari and six non-resident ambassadors from Albania, Azerbaijan, Côte d’Ivoire, Iceland, Georgia,...

item.title: Third Bunge Marathon in Dodoma to Attract Regional Runners
item.description: The third edition of the Bunge Marathon, scheduled for April 18 in Dodoma, has been upgraded to international level, attracting participation from runners across the region. The President of the Tanza...

item.title: Ontario plans long-term jail expansion amid overcrowding concerns
item.description: Ontario is facing a significant challenge with overcrowded jails, and plans to add nearly 6,000 beds by 2050, according to documents obtained through freedom-of-information requests. The expansion wil...

item.title: Lawyer says Duterte impeachment complainants express satisfaction with Madriaga’s testimony
item.description: Supporters of the impeachment complaint against former President Rodrigo Duterte have expressed their satisfaction with the recent testimony given by witness Madriaga, according to their legal counsel...

item.title: Diplomatic Efforts Intensify as Israel and Lebanon Begin Talks Amidst Regional Tensions
item.description: Amidst ongoing conflict and heightened tensions in the Middle East, international efforts to de-escalate the situation between Israel and Lebanon are gaining momentum. Diplomats from both nations have...

item.title: Emfuleni begins clearing dumping sites after public pressure and media exposé
item.description: Emfuleni Local Municipality has started clearing one of its worst dumping sites after widespread criticism and media attention over the worsening waste crisis in the area. The clean-up began a day aft...

item.title: Cignal advances to PVL Finals after win over Creamline, PLDT eliminated
item.description: Cignal Super Spikers booked their place in the Premier Volleyball League (PVL) Finals after a hard-fought victory over the Creamline Cool Smashers, effectively ending PLDT’s hopes of staying in conten...

item.title: Expanded 2026 FIFA World Cup Expected to Drive Major Growth in Global Sports Betting Market
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is expected to be the biggest football tournament ever, both in size and economic impact. With an estimated global audience of more than 5 billion viewers, the tournament conti...

item.title: Eight arrested in Sopore after protests over teacher’s alleged sexual assault on student
item.description: The Jammu and Kashmir Police have arrested eight individuals for vandalism during demonstrations at a government higher secondary school in Sopore, Baramulla district. The protests erupted after alleg...

item.title: Hungary’s Viktor Orbán concedes defeat as opposition Tisza party claims victory
item.description: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán has accepted a 'painful but clear' defeat after the opposition Tisza party, led by Péter Magyar, won a landslide victory in the national parliamentary elections. ...

item.title: Wholesale cost reductions could lead to lower fuel prices
item.description: Recent trends in the fuel market suggest that motorists may see relief at the pump in the coming weeks. Wholesale fuel costs, which have a significant impact on the price consumers pay, are showing si...

item.title: President Murmu calls for inclusive society beyond caste divisions on Ambedkar Jayanti
item.description: President Droupadi Murmu, speaking at the Samajik Samrasta Mahotsav held in Gandhinagar on the occasion of B. R. Ambedkar’s birth anniversary, called upon citizens to move beyond caste divisions and w...

item.title: Maduka Okoye Reflect On Udinese’s Dominant 3-0 Win Over AC Milan At San Siro
item.description: Udinese goalkeeper Maduka Okoye talk about how special their 3-0 win against AC Milan be for all the players, saying e go remain unforgettable for the whole team. The match wey happen for the famous S...

item.title: Mudavadi Receives Report to Improve Management of Diplomatic Privileges and UN Relations in Kenya
item.description: Prime Cabinet Secretary and Cabinet Secretary for Foreign and Diaspora Affairs, Musalia Mudavadi, has officially received a comparative study report that focuses on enhancing the administration and ma...

item.title: 87-year-old bushwalker survives cold night in WA bushland after getting lost
item.description: An 87-year-old woman has survived a harsh, near-freezing night in the south-west of Western Australia after becoming lost while bushwalking. The elderly hiker, whose name has not been released, was re...
